text: Had a fairly good sized tree succumb to the wet and wind and fall over two days ago. Emailed Chapala Tree Service, they responded immediately and came quickly to look at, emailed me in a couple hours with a very reasonable quote to completely remove and came out the same day, yesterday, and did a very good job including thorough cleanup.
title: Can't Say Enough Good About Chapala Tree Service

text: When Roberto came to collect this morning they had cut a section of the tree out that had two orchids on it without being asked. I just assumed the orchids wouldn't surve the process but they made sure they did.
title: Can't Say Enough Good About Chapala Tree Service

text: I just can't say enough good about Chapala Tree Service.  Great atttitude, quality of work, reliability, fair and honest pricing, they have it all.  Thumbs up for this great local company.
title: Can't Say Enough Good About Chapala Tree Service
Can't Say Enough Good About Chapala Tree Service

text: Matteo Volpi is a project leader for Senderos y Caminos de México A.C., which was founded in Ajijic in 2014 to help people find their way along Mexico’s vast network of rural trails, and also to rescue, rehabilitate and preserve these ancient footpaths, some of which were in use long before the arrival of the Conquistadores.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: Matteo Volpi shows the official Senderos trail marker—a bright orange triangle—to rancher Felipe Callejas, who lives deep inside the forest between Mascota and Puerto Vallarta.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: In 2021, Senderos published a 144-page Trail Guide for de los Membrillos. It comes in both a Spanish and an English version and contains 100 color photos as well as 26 maps featuring contour lines, estimates of hiking time and levels of difficulty.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: One of the organization’s newest projects, Matteo Volpi told me, is what Senderos will call the Chapala Trail Network.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: “It’s a project we’ve been working on for ten years. There are, of course, a great many trails above the lake shore, but what we want to do is to create a mini network inside the bigger network.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: “We want to get at least six trails into really good shape and then have five different axes, heading to Chupinaya, El Caracol, Ixtlalhuacan, La Cañada and to Las Trojes.  So it will look like a spider, and people can say, ‘I’ll start in El Caracol and finish in Ajijic’ or ‘I can start in Ajijic and finish up in La Cañada,’ or you can create your own trail.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: “To renovate and signpost each leg of the spider, we’ll be working very closely with the local community.”
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: Perhaps Senderos de Mexico’s most ambitious project is a 70-kilometer-long trail that starts in Mascota at an altitude of 1,300 meters and takes trekkers down to Playa Grande Ecopark on the Pacific Coast, near Puerto Vallarta.
title: Reviving Mexico’s rural trails,  Chapala, Ajijic, Jocotepec, and Ixlahuácan y mas

text: Currently, the electromobility project is in an operational testing phase on the main corridor; technicians are verifying travel times and operational accuracy.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: The system will operate with a fleet of 41 Volvo Luminus BZR buses, which are 100% electric.So far, eight of these vehicles are already in the city for preliminary testing. Each unit has a capacity to transport 80 passengers. They have air conditioning, free wifi, for a first-world transportation system that will connect to the Guadalajara airport.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: To reinforce the system's operation, new articulated 7800 Electric units, zero-emission options for urban mobility, will also arrive in the city in the coming days.. According to authorities, these buses are equipped with high-performance dual electric motors and batteries up to 720 kWh. They offer extended range, energy efficiency, and a fast-charging system of up to 250 kW.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: Designed for large cities, they have a capacity for 130 passengers.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: The entrance to the city presented a significant challenge, with an investment of approximately 11 billion pesos in state funds to completely transform the urban landscape at the airport entrance.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: They have widened this access road to 12 lanes, added hydraulic concrete, a bike path, street lights, and trees, which will give the city a new look, they are also working to improve the visual appearance of the airport.
title: Pablo Lemus announces the opening of Line 5 on June 4th

text: Visit this event organized by local beekeepers and get a lot of honey-derived products and foods. For three days you can discover 100% local honey and enjoy cultural events about bees and hive products, as part of World Bee Day , which is celebrated every May 20th. It will take place from May 23rd to 25th , from 10:00 AM to 8:00 PM , in San Nicolás de Bari Park , in front of the sanctuary.
title: Guadalajara Honey Fair

text:
Admission is free. Organized by the Local Livestock Association of Beekeepers of Guadalajara , this fair seeks to promote local consumption and highlight the importance of bees for the balance of the ecosystem. Among the products you will find are:
	
	Honey from different flowers
	Pollen and propolis
	Mead
	Handmade sweets
	Natural soaps, creams and shampoos
title: Guadalajara Honey Fair

text: Are you receiving messages about traffic tickets? It's a scam!
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: The Mexico City Secretariat of Citizen Security (SSC) warned of a digital fraud in which cybercriminal groups use websites that impersonate official government platforms to steal users' personal and financial data.
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: A form of fraud is receiving messages about supposed fines with malicious links.
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: The  authorities warned that information entered on fake websites can be used for identity theft or financial fraud, urging citizens not to enter personal data , passwords, or banking information on unofficial or dubious websites.
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: The Cyber Police of the SSC recommended that users verify that domains have the .gob.mx extension and the https security certificate as an initial filter of authenticity.
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: Source: Aristegui Noticias
title: Text messages about traffic fines

text: More speeding tickets soon: 7 speed cameras to be installed on Lázaro Cárdenas and the Chapala Highway
title: More speed cameras

text: The Metropolitan Infrastructure Agency for Mobility ( AMIM ) confirmed the installation of seven new photo-fine radars at strategic points in the Guadalajara Metropolitan Area.
title: More speed cameras

text: The devices will be placed on Lázaro Cárdenas Avenue , where there will be three units, and on the Chapala Highway, with four more in the section that goes from the Periférico to the Guadalajara International Airport, reported Antonio Martín del Campo , general administrator of AMIM.
title: More speed cameras

text: Source: UGTV44
title: More speed cameras

text: After seeing a Neuorologist for a 3 minute consultation and prescription for 1400p, I had an appointment with Optometrist Salvador Martinez in Ajijic. I brought in my glasses, expecting a new prescription. Salvador spent 30 minutes with me and told me Honestly that the glasses I have are too close to a new prescription.
title: Great Optometrist

text: I think many would have given me a slightly different prescription and charged me. But Salvador told me truthfully that it wouldn't help. All this for 400p. I Highly Reccommend this Optometrist!
title: Great Optometrist

text: Salvador Borrayo Martinex, Hidalgo 6A, Ajijic  376-766-5340
title: Great Optometrist

text: A merchant from the Chapala Lakeside, known in Ajijic as "Berry Man", was affected after the blockades and burning of vehicles registered on Sunday in different parts of Jalisco.
title: Local looses truck to narcos

text: According to witnesses, he went to the Central de Abastos market that morning to stock up on merchandise for sale. However, during the violence that included road blockades and the burning of vehicles in various municipalities, his truck was intercepted. According to reports, he was forced out of the vehicle, and the truck was set on fire along with the goods he was transporting.
title: Local looses truck to narcos

text: Following the incident, neighbors and customers have begun to spread the word about a volunteer support initiative to help him recover.
title: Local looses truck to narcos

title: Local looses truck to narcos

title: Local looses truck to narcos

text:
Online Disinformation Fueled Panic After The Killing Of Mexico's Most Powerful Drug Lord
	By María Verza and Megan Janetsky
	AP The Associated Press
	February 25, 2026
title: Recent Cartel Violence in Mexico - Impact of Online Disinformation

text: .  When unrest broke-out in Mexico on February 22nd, the internet was flooded with disinformation designed to stoke fear.
title: Recent Cartel Violence in Mexico - Impact of Online Disinformation

text: .  The government said there were between 200 and 500 inaccurate posts following Sunday's army operation.
title: Recent Cartel Violence in Mexico - Impact of Online Disinformation

text: .  One fake post reported that gunmen had seized Guadalajara’s international airport and tourists had been taken hostage.
title: Recent Cartel Violence in Mexico - Impact of Online Disinformation

text: .  "We didn’t know what was true and what was false," said Guadalajara resident Victoria Elizabeth Peceril.
title: Recent Cartel Violence in Mexico - Impact of Online Disinformation

text: This was posted on Facebook by Chapala.
title: Update from Chapala about reporting abandoned junk cars and getting them removed.

text: Strengthen removal of abandoned vehicles in Chapala
title: Update from Chapala about reporting abandoned junk cars and getting them removed.

text: D. Arthur Ortega. - In recent times, the Mobility Directorate of Chapala has strengthened the permanent work to remove abandoned vehicles from the public road, both at the municipal headquarters and in their delegations, reported its owner, Francisco Moisés Ortiz de la Rosa.
title: Update from Chapala about reporting abandoned junk cars and getting them removed.

text: The official clarified that this is not an extraordinary campaign, but an everyday work that has been given more attention due to the disruptions generated by abandoned cars. According to official data, from July to December 2025, 42 vehicles were removed that, after being notified, were not relocated by their owners within the stipulated time frame.
title: Update from Chapala about reporting abandoned junk cars and getting them removed.

text: Ortiz de la Rosa explained that the procedure begins when the unit receives a citizen report. Mobility personnel check if the vehicle shows signs of abandonment, such as excessive dirt, punctured tires or other conditions and, if confirmed, place a fine and a notification giving the owner 15 days to remove or relocate it.
title: Update from Chapala about reporting abandoned junk cars and getting them removed.

text: If the deadline is due without response, a crane is requested to move the unit to the corral.
title: Update from Chapala about reporting abandoned junk cars and getting them removed.

text: To recover a removed vehicle, the owner must pay the corresponding fine and credit the property to the Mobility Management. The fine rises to 3 UMAS, with 50 percent discount if settled within the first five working days. Currently, the value of the Unit of Measurement and Update (UMA) is 117.31 pesos per day, 3 thousand 566.22 pesos per month and 42 thousand 794.64 pesos per year.
title: Update from Chapala about reporting abandoned junk cars and getting them removed.

text: The director emphasized the importance of keeping the roads free of abandoned vehicles, as they generate visual pollution, favor the proliferation of harmful wildlife and reduce public spaces available to citizens and tourism.
title: Update from Chapala about reporting abandoned junk cars and getting them removed.

text: Finally, he invited the population to report these types of cases. Reports can be made directly to the Mobility Directorate at the telephone 376 688 1935, or via WhatsApp to the Citizen's Service number 33 3227 4444, sending a photograph of the vehicle and its location.
title: Update from Chapala about reporting abandoned junk cars and getting them removed.

text: Everything I've ever wanted in 28 years of waiting. It turned a 12 hour travel trip into 5 hours which makes me very happy. There is no first class or business class but there is a lot of leg room which I appreciated. Seats are not spectacularly comfortable and I would suggest a pillow or some kind of neck support.
title: Report on Flair Airline direct flight Guadalajara to Toronto

text: Nuthin's free these days and you can only pay for food or beverages with a credit card...no cash. Don't order the individual, very mediocre pizza. If you do not print out your boarding pass, you will be charged another $25.00 Canadian for standing in their check in line. I was disappointed to find that their "drop off luggage line" did not exist at either end of this trip.
title: Report on Flair Airline direct flight Guadalajara to Toronto

text: You are standing in line with all the other people who did not print out their boarding passes which was a tedious waste of time. You do get nickel and dimed for 1) checked baggage 2) advance seat choice and 3) priority boarding. Even with those added, it was still the cheapest and fastest travel to Toronto and back that I've experienced while living full time in Mexico.
title: Report on Flair Airline direct flight Guadalajara to Toronto

text: I will definitely choose them again. There is also a direct flight Guadalajara to Vancouver for those interested.
title: Report on Flair Airline direct flight Guadalajara to Toronto

text: I recently went shopping at Pancho's for some of those oddball things that are a little hard to get since, well, they're odd. Normally, I only go in for a paper and one or two items, but yesterday I decided to do a little exploring since they've expanded. Wow, it's definitely worth the trip!
title: Pancho's supermarket

text: Before, the place always seemed a bit cramped and not well organized, but now that it's expanded into two sides, it's all super-clean and tidy. There was a small, but varied selection of frozen vegan & vegetarian meals, lots of veggies, decent amount of frozen goods, and the usual imports and/or Costco run items.
title: Pancho's supermarket

text: I wasn't much of a fan when it first opened, but if you haven't been there in a while, I really recommend it. The only negative (and it's not a biggie) is walking between the diners when you go from one section to the other.
title: Pancho's supermarket

text: The restaurant section was quite busy, so I think I'll give it another try. If it matches the shopping, well, they'll have another loyal customer.
title: Pancho's supermarket

text: I went to WW early this morning because I knew it would be quiet -- still an unorganized mess with stock in the aisles -- but at least it was a quiet mess. My cart was loaded and I headed to check out. Lo and behold, although there were a number of staff, NOT ONE CASHIER was available. Every single person had to go through the self-check out.
title: Sunday Adventures at Wally's World

text: The girl monitoring the area saw my cart and waved me on because...surprise!...no cashier in place. It took me three separate transactions to get everything checked out. I had to place my bags on the floor in order to get things on the scale. To be very frank, the place is one giant, unorganized cluster f***.
title: Sunday Adventures at Wally's World

text: The only shiny moment was the girl in that section who was patient, friendly, and handled a bad situation well. There was plenty of staff to spare one to open a lane, but did that happen? No. They didn't open up a manned lane until 0930. Is anyone actually in charge there? It seems like most of the employees are on their phones, chatting, or just hiding; pick one.
title: Sunday Adventures at Wally's World

text: Interestingly, I then went to Superlake which was also quiet. When I went to check out, they had 3 lanes open. In. Out. Easy peasy. Hmmmmm...
title: Sunday Adventures at Wally's World

text: BTW, I know this little rant probably won't change a thing, but it would be nice if someone in management actually managed. I'm not holding out hope, though.
title: Sunday Adventures at Wally's World

text: I don't reach out like I used to because I don't like getting slammed when I try to help BUT here I go again.
title: Beware of CAREGIVERS

text: I have witnessed what happens when you open your hearts and wallets to a trusted CAREGIVER that should not be trusted. There are wonderful people out there and there are grifters as well. I suggest, and of course you can ignore me, a backup person to check on the caregivers.  Stealing is too easy.
title: Beware of CAREGIVERS

text: Hard to miss the man on carretera just before Walmart T-intersection light who politely hopes for a few pesos. Bent over right angle at hips, special crutches,  always with floppy sun hat and long-sleeved jacket. What I saw was a person trying to bring a few pesos into his home, thinking  his apparent physical condition makes work impossible.
title: A Hero Hiding in Plain Sight

text: But instead of staying home (poor me ☹️) as many might he's out there in all seasons, bad weather or not, and  never without a cheery smile. I've made it my mission for some time to give him something every Tuesday on my "east" trip.
title: A Hero Hiding in Plain Sight

text: I was super worried when, larger "Christmas" amount in hand, he wasn't there as usual Dec 8, 9, or 11. Fretted all week. Health? Injury? Worse?  So  was thrilled when yesterday, Dec 16 he was back at his post. Couldn't stop on way to iShop, nor on return to enter into Walmart(lights uncooperative). But as often, I "mouthed" al regresar and he smiled and waved, this being our custom.
title: A Hero Hiding in Plain Sight

text: OH NO! Coming out of Walmart I don't see him????? Then spot him sitting on curb under a tree facing closed restaurant. Sudden right turn into lot and park close by. Walk over and ask if he's OK. (All conversations in Spanish). Thank you señora, It's so hot I just needed shade for a bit. Don't worry. Then tell him I WAS very worried when he wasn't there last week.
title: A Hero Hiding in Plain Sight

text: He profoundly apologizes to ME for causing My worries--- three times!! And now comes the HERO bit (condensed version).
title: A Hero Hiding in Plain Sight

text: What happened? I was studying really hard for first semester university exams. WOW! I think to myself..... THAT was unexpected. What year? Oh señora, only 1st semester, first year. I had to wait to save up enough  for fees. How old are you? 24.....lot younger than he seems as you whiz by but up close it's  evident. What subject? Mechanical engineer. I want to be very qualified to work on cars!!!
title: A Hero Hiding in Plain Sight

text: WOW again! ( I'm thinking tech institute but doesn't lessen  the achievement). He was more than grateful for my larger, but not huge addition to his coffers.
title: A Hero Hiding in Plain Sight

text: His name is Marco. He needs more help than I can give. For those that DO donate, now you know more of his story. For those who don't, this guy really deserves being SEEN and not just ignored as you drive by.    He doesn't know or need to about this post. Better he just is thankful for any more support than in the past.   GRACIAS!!!
title: A Hero Hiding in Plain Sight

text: Chapala launches security operation for the holiday period
title: Extra security for holidays lakeside

text: From December 12 to January 7 , the Chapala Government will launch the "Safe Holidays, Guadalupe – Reyes 2025 " security operation, in order to reinforce surveillance in the municipality during the holiday period with the highest tourist influx.
title: Extra security for holidays lakeside

text: The municipal police, civil protection and firefighters, the State Security Secretariat, the Mexican Army and the National Guard will participate in the operations.
title: Extra security for holidays lakeside

text: Mayor Alejandro Aguirre inaugurated the security operation and stated that it aims to guarantee the safety of residents and tourists during the Christmas and New Year's holidays. "In Chapala, we know that security is a job that is built every day, and that it can only be achieved through effective coordination among the three levels of government.
title: Extra security for holidays lakeside

text: Therefore, during this period, we will reinforce the preventive presence in tourist areas, boardwalks, highways, high-traffic areas, and access points to the municipality."
title: Extra security for holidays lakeside

text: As part of the security operation, the mayor handed over the keys to the new module to the State Police's Amphibious Squadron , located on the waterfront.
title: Extra security for holidays lakeside

text: "Today we hand over the keys, but we also reaffirm our commitment to continue working in coordination with the State of Jalisco on the security and protection of our lake, a natural treasure that gives us our identity and that we must preserve for future generations," he said.
title: Extra security for holidays lakeside

text: Hernández González indicated that this squadron is in charge of maritime search and rescue actions, special and tactical operations, hydrographic reconnaissance and other tasks in aquatic environments and lake shore
title: Extra security for holidays lakeside

text: Source: El Informador.
title: Extra security for holidays lakeside

text: My wife and I moved into an assisted living house in Riberas 6 months ago, owned and operated by Dr. Gerardo Leon (333-310-0927 ). We have had a very positive experience. The food is above average, the staff is attentive, most amenities are provided, and we receive concierge level medical care. Of course we are subject to the infrastructure problems of Riberas, but all in all a good experience.
title: Assisted living in Riberas, our experience

text: We highly recommend it.
title: Assisted living in Riberas, our experience

text: Last year two men were going door to door appearing to be garbage men and asking for the Christmas bonus. Give envelope only to men with the truck. If possible take a photo of the men. When they see the camera they run off.
title: Beware Fake Trash Men

text: We have been using Miguel (Father) and Jorge (Son) Jimenez for several years now for problems large and small. They are not only very good at their jobs, but they are also two of the nicest, most honest tradesmen you'll meet. They come when they say they'll come, get the job done and do it well, and don't charge "gringo" prices.
title: EXCELLENT Plumber/Electricians

text: They do electrical work, plumbing, gas (we've used them for those three and have been very happy), and more. You can contact Jorge on WhatsApp 331 323 6180. ETA:  while I've always spoken w/them in (barely tolerable) Spanish, it's been pointed out in a posting (below) that Jorge speaks pretty English. That's really good to know!
title: EXCELLENT Plumber/Electricians

text: I've seen several people ask this question , but no answers; now I have one.
title: Open MRI in Guadalajara

text: I just used PSH Imagen, near the Minerva circle, (Address: Diag. Golfo de Cortés 4114, Monraz, 44670 Guadalajara, Jal.) for an open MRI on my back. I was able to make an appointment on What's App (next day), and had back-to-back MRIs (thoracic and lumbar) and I was out in one hour forty-five minutes.
title: Open MRI in Guadalajara

text: They have no English language capability, but were very friendly and willing to explain what was going to happen. The cost was $4600 MXP per MRI. This is a picture of the Open MRI they use:
title: Open MRI in Guadalajara

text: You can find more info on FaceBook.  I'll come back and post what my doctor thought of the quality of the report shortly.
title: Open MRI in Guadalajara

text: Gas workers across the country are threatening to stage a national strike in the first week of June . They are demanding a 4.24 peso increase in the price of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG).
title: FILL YOUR LPG TANK IN MAY

text: They asked President Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo and Energy Secretary Luz Elena González to convene a meeting specifically to reach agreements with the energy sector.
title: FILL YOUR LPG TANK IN MAY

text: Legal LP gas operators reported that the price cap, established in 2021 , has led to unsustainable operating costs, resulting in the dismissal of workers.
title: FILL YOUR LPG TANK IN MAY

text:
Social Security Payments Suspended for Americans Broad
	Story by Hannah Bietz, Independent New Reporter
	Published on the MSN News web portal
	February 5, 2025
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: The Social Security Administration (SSA) has suspended payments to some Americans living abroad. This is because they did not submit a required form called the Foreign Enforcement Questionnaire. The form is needed each year to confirm eligibility for payments outside the U.S.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: The suspensions impact Americans who receive Social Security benefits and have an address in another country.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: According to the SSA, over 700,000 beneficiaries live in foreign countries as of December 2023. Around 59,000 live in Mexico and 2,000 in Costa Rica. U.S. Embassies in several countries have sent out notices about the suspended payments.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: The U.S. Embassy in Costa Rica stated, “If you did not receive your February benefit payment, your benefits have been suspended.” Similar messages went out to those in Mexico, Haiti, and other nations. Not all Americans abroad are affected right away.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: Those under 90 who receive their own benefits and have a Social Security Number ending in 50 to 99 do not need to submit the form now.  They will get a form to complete between June and July 2025.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: Those with a U.S. address on record are also not required to respond.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: If your Social Security payment for February did not arrive, your benefits were likely suspended. Payments started going out on January 31, with the last ones issued by February 28. To get benefits reinstated, you must contact the Federal Benefits Unit for your country.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: For Costa Rica, call 506-2519-2228 Monday to Thursday from 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m., or email FBU.CostaRica@ssa.gov. In Mexico, email FBU.Mexico.City@ssa.gov, FBU.Guadalajara@ssa.gov, or FBU.Ciudad.Juarez@ssa.gov. Submit the appropriate Foreign Enforcement Questionnaire or confirm your compliance.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: Once the unit receives the necessary documents, benefits should be reinstated within about seven business days. The SSA sends these questionnaires to beneficiaries outside the U.S. every year or two. The answers help determine ongoing eligibility for benefits.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: If you do not receive the form when expected, contact the SSA or your Federal Benefits Unit.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/news/social-security-payments-suspended-for-americans-abroad/ar-AA1yBKXc#:~:text=The Social Security Administration,for payments outside the U.S.
title: Monthly Social Security check - More

text: I'm a few days late, but I just wanted to give a big shout out and even bigger "Thanks!" to the gentleman who usually has a food stand in front of Pancho's on Saturdays (gringo). I was at the cash register paying for our purchases when hubs wanted to return to the car to sit and wait for me. As he was walking out and down the slight slope, his left leg gave out on him and he nearly fell.
title: Shout out to the guy in front of Pancho's

text: The guy saw it happening and immediately rushed over to help. It may seem like a small action to some, but it was a big one to us. Due to a rapidly deteriorating injury and age, walking is becoming more and more challenging for hubs. Falls are a real concern.
title: Shout out to the guy in front of Pancho's

text: If you're the gentleman who helped my husband, THANK YOU!
title: Shout out to the guy in front of Pancho's

text: A couple of nights ago, hubs received a call on his cell. He answered but couldn't understand the woman who was sobbing and babbling in Spanish. He could only understand "Papa! Papa!" between the excessive bouts of crying. He passed it to me, and I told her she had the wrong number (as far as he knows, he has no daughters  ). Nope. She sniveled and sobbed while burbling who-knows-what.
title: New Year, Old Scam

text: Then, she passed the phone to some serious-sounding guy who identified himself as Comandante Garbled of Garblelandia. I kept repeating that I didn't understand him, and he kept repeating his stern message. The only thing I got out of it was "secuestra" (kidnap). So, I repeated that I didn't understand him three or four more times, but now in English before hanging up.
title: New Year, Old Scam

text: Yes, the phone scams are alive and well here in Lakeside. Fortunately, I didn't say the name of someone because the woman would have latched onto it and said she was "X," and then Garbled would have gotten on to say that she had been kidnapped and we owed "X" if we wanted to see her again.
title: New Year, Old Scam

text: Usually, there's a very good English speaker involved, but I guess we got the knock-off version who didn't realize the number belonged to a gringo.
title: New Year, Old Scam

text: I know most folks can spot these scams, but you'd be amazed at just how good they are at extracting information from you without you even realizing it. Just be careful because I guess some folks find this an easy way to pay for their Xmas extravaganzas.
title: New Year, Old Scam

text: Anything obscuring  ALL four corners of license plates ( new or old) subject to huge fines beginning  Thurs, Jan 9, all over Jalisco. This includes most, if not all, car dealership plate holders plus other vanity things, not to mention any other way of making license plates difficult/impossible to read.
title: BIG Traffic Fines Starting Thurs Jan 9

text: Note in the following illustration ( from Informador) that the entire license plate with all those little details MUST be completely visilble.
title: BIG Traffic Fines Starting Thurs Jan 9

title: Happy New Year!

text: Here's a phrase I used to hear often over time but now never do: "Oh, you don't look it!"
title: Just thinkin'

text: Has your age caught up with you, too? What the heck. Toujour gai!
title: Just thinkin'

text: And Happy New Year
title: Just thinkin'

text: This morning 2 men came and rang my bell. Picked up my garbage and moved it away. Waved white envelope and wanted Christmas tip. I asked where the truck was and they tried to say it was around the corner. I asked why they were early instead of 4pm and they said their posada was in the afternoon. I had the tip in my hand and he kept reaching for it.
title: Beware Garbage Men Scam

text: They were clean and wearing decent clothes with red vests and gloves. Did not appear to be working at collecting the garbage. I refused to give them the envelope. He kept yelling at me as I turned and went back into the house.
title: Beware Garbage Men Scam

text: My usual truck came at 4 and I told them what happened. They asked for someone to take photos. I was happy to give them the gift envelope and hope many do not fall for these two bandits.
title: Beware Garbage Men Scam

text: Others, have shared their experiences with us.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: They do not want to share with their community for a variety of reasons.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: They feel this voicing of their experience, would be unpopular or unwanted.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: Yet, I wonder how many of you take exception to that manner of thinking,
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: and would like to share (without naming names etc) your experience.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: Our story:
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: For many years we depended upon higher end Physicians in The Lake Chapala area.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: We spared nothing in regards to timely /regular assessments and testing/procedures.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: Funds were available and allocated for our Medical Care.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: We were given clean bills of health, and even consulted our US Cardio Specialist, when at one point a Stent was unnecessarily recommended.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: Forward this approach to a family member insisting that we have a redo or medical evaluation in the US during a visit there.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: What that unearthed was:
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: A late diagnosis of prostate cancer, which would have had a different outcome had diagnosis come earlier.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: An undiagnosed situation requiring treatment for Pre diabetes, and the beginning of kidney disease, also would have
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: been treated earlier.  The doctor in the kidney disease, ignored the blood / urine test results. The US doctor jumped on them
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: and was appalled at how long they had been on a decline.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: For the other half of this couple:
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: A misdiagnosis in Mexico (under stating that as such) with that person being told she needed emergency surgery for a hiatal hernia,
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: which turned out to not exist in the slightest.  The Doctor sent up a red flag when he was concerned that she would be getting a second
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: opinion in the US, and exclaimed... "But you said you don't have US Health insurance!"
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: We TRULY believed what we were told by others in our community.  We were in great hands and had the best care
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: available.  One of us has paid a severe cost in regards to a shortening of desirable outcome.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: We and others like ourselves, who we have spoken to about our Lake Chapala/Guadalajara Medical experience, are questioning why
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: other residents only want to say 'GOOD' things about Medical care in Mexico.  I have spoken with three others who had late cancer
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: diagnosis, and only became aware due to a visit to the U.S./ Canada.  We can only think of one reason.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: People do not want to come across as negative or the Ugly American/Canadian, so they say only positive things about Mexico.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: This really puts people at extreme risk.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: It has cut to the core across other areas, such as safety which is non medical, and even risk, to ourselves/our pets.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: In the midst of all of this, we actually were confronted with a 'death diagnosis' for one of our dogs, from a top physician,
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: one of the most well know Vets in the Lake Chapala are (shall go unnamed in this post).
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: At this point we drove all the way to Texas, and yes, unlike what we were told in Mexico, with one of us believing this trip
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: was in vain.... As a result this dog's life was saved.  We felt horrible. Yes we had saved this sweet dog.  But we had previously at the same vet's advice, gave
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: a death shot to another pet, poor thing.  Things like this add up, and can cause long term trauma.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: You are left with a trust broken, that actually may have not been put in place, if not for all the rave reviews on medical care.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: Yes, this is rather discouraging.  We'd think that it is only us, but we have since spoken with at least 10 people who had a
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: similar experience.  I have had widows/widowers who have come up to me, and said this was the most horrid experience
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: and then told me they should have sought care NOB.  All you can do is listen at that point and sympathize.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: But here, going forward, it may not make us popular, but at least in disclosing what we have learned, we stand to be better
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: friends and give more accurate information, so that others can protect themselves.
title: Attn Expats: What are your experiences with: Misdiagnosis / Lack of Diagnosis

text: Important differences so you don't get sold on an incorrect resolution. I have red ants which were disturbed when I installed a sump pump. Although I admire their industrious work ethic, they are a P.I.T.A. . I moved my fridge a couple of weeks ago to clean behind it. OMG! They had entered through a small crack at the base of the wall and there was a MOUNTAIN of sand and debris.
title: Are they red ants or termite tunnels?

text: They weren't there when I went to Canada in September. So, I'm posting this because I wanted an old cabinet base removed and was told I have termites. NOT! Know the difference or your pocket book will feel the pinch (no pun intended although red ants do "bite") There was absolutely no evidence of wood damage to the base drawer frame.
title: Are they red ants or termite tunnels?

text: https://pestcemetery.com/the-mysterious-above-ground-ant-tunnels/
title: Are they red ants or termite tunnels?

text: I know his info was posted some time ago, and SO glad I copied it down. My washer refused to "agitate" yesterday and with 8 years under its belt, was not surprised.  Called Gualberto.  He agreed to come right over (oh no, señora, no extra charge to come today ---Saturday) since I told him I  have to go to Guad on Monday.
title: Kudos to Gualberto and Omar

text: He and son Omar showed up in less than an hour (Chapala to SAT) and quickly diagnosed problem as being a "little plastic part" under the base of the agitator post.  No big deal and he can get it and come back Monday when I get back from the city.
title: Kudos to Gualberto and Omar

text: While he was here, I asked about  2 cycles that have never worked since I bought the (new) machine, and was always too lazy to check while under warranty so you know how much I cared.  This would be a much more expensive repair as the whole electronic  "unit" behind the selector dial would need replacing.  We both agreed if I haven't needed them in 8 years I can live without them for a lot longer!
title: Kudos to Gualberto and Omar

text: Refused any money.... "oh no señora, you only pay when I am done". And was very honest and up-front with me when some might have tried to take advantage and find something much more expensive to fix. VERY pleased and want to give him lots of praise for initiative and prompt service.
title: Kudos to Gualberto and Omar

text: I originally posted this before the great website meltdown and the posting disappeared.
title: Great Computer Repairman-does house calls!

text: In short, if you need a really good computer tech who does house calls without charging you everything in your wallet, we highly recommend Felipe de Jesus Miranda D. I won't point fingers for obvious reasons, but we've had a lot of problems with other techs.
title: Great Computer Repairman-does house calls!

text: Anyway, Felipe got my laptop up and running within 20-30 minutes when another guy sat on it for over a week and then asked me why it was there when I went back for it! (no, I didn't pay). It's been several months and the computer still runs great.
title: Great Computer Repairman-does house calls!

text: FWIW, yes, I realize that I'm not tech-savvy and it could be two different problems that I'm comparing, but the thing in common is the level of professionalism. Hub's laptop was about as DOA as it could get and Felipe got that up and running, too. His prices were very reasonable, especially for a house call, and he definitely knows his "stuff."
title: Great Computer Repairman-does house calls!

text: miranda608@hotmail.com     331 574 8896
title: Great Computer Repairman-does house calls!

text: Last night, at around 10:30 pm, someone knocked on my outer door to the street. I quietly locked my house door and cracked open the dining room window to listen and observe. They knocked again and I yelled "Who is it? Quien es?" Some guy said his name and I said "No te conozco. (I don't know you)". He said "Gracias" and left.
title: Heads up!

text: This morning, my neighbour asked me if I enjoyed my pizza because they had tried his place first. It was our morning exchange to acknowledge that we're both still kicking. I told him that I didn't order a pizza and that I didn't open my door to anyone that I wasn't expecting (not even the chat port). It could have been an address error but I thought it prudent to give you a heads up. Take care.
title: Heads up!

text: My maid, who empties the trash baskets at my local bank, say the peso may hit 20 to 1 very soon. She also predicted there will be less rain after the end of October. She doesn't know if my girl friend really loves me....but my dog is looking into that. She also says that the "RobinCops are back working the stretch of highway from San Juan to Jocotepec on both sides of the road and my dog agrees.
title: Mexican Peso to USD and the RobinCops

text: Try to drive in packs of cars as they like to stop individuals driving alone.
title: Mexican Peso to USD and the RobinCops

text: Edited October 10, 2024 by BenjiBoy
title: Mexican Peso to USD and the RobinCops

text: I was stopped outside of Jocotepec by the police last night...."We need to see your TARJETA"" It is outdated" the clown cop said and of course it was not outdated , I had it, "License," I had it and it was still good...."We need to see your insurance policy"...They said it was not valid.
title: New Police Rip-OFF

text: "We need to see that you paid the tax registration on this vehicle," I did not have it nor did I know that I needed to carry it," you were speeding and crossed a double line...so we will need to tow you."" How can I get home? WALK or get someone to come get you.
title: New Police Rip-OFF

text: Plus we are giving you tickets for each offence and you can't take anything out of your car nor can you get it back before Monday and it may cost up to 10000mxp or more."
title: New Police Rip-OFF

text: I called my insurance agent and he assured them my policy was correct and pd for....they wouldn't take his word for that. Even though he sent a fresh copy over my cell phone I called my well-connected attorney...they said" we never heard of him." Even though he was the former city attorney.
title: New Police Rip-OFF

text: I called the former city manager....my neighbor....".Okay!!!" the female cop says, in poor english, "I know him"..."how much money do you have?" I said 700pesos...."not enough!!! "But we will take it," and they did. No ticket, no tow...2 hours wasted.
title: New Police Rip-OFF

text: "Now before your neighbor gets here...get out of here!!!!."" They told my neighbor when he arrived to drive me home that I had left...He asked if I left without permission and they admitted that I was allowed to go.
title: New Police Rip-OFF

text: What will I do about this? Feel sorry for myself, for sure...put the "original of my tax receipt and a fresh copy of the same insurance policy" that I showed them in my car and try to forget about it. Crooks are everywhere and this was cheaper than having my throat cut for my knock off Rolex on a NY street.
title: New Police Rip-OFF

text: I tell about this so some of you may avoid this scam....carry everything,  unstained, unfolded and even if unnecessary. Were they within their rights to do this....probably not but could they be stopped....NO. I had no defence except to offer the requested bribe and I am lucky that it was enough to let me go home. I still love my life here and lucky for me this hasn't happened to me before.
title: New Police Rip-OFF

text: Will I ever help the local police again....I doubt it.
title: New Police Rip-OFF

text: Cofepris warns about counterfeiting of type 2 diabetes medications
title: Check Diabetes type 2 medicine

text: The Federal Commission for the Protection against Sanitary Risks (Cofepris) warns about the falsification and irregular marketing of medicines to treat type 2 diabetes.
title: Check Diabetes type 2 medicine

text: The company Boehringer Ingelheim Promeco notified Profeco of the falsification of batches D49752, D53476, D53472, D55738, D53477, D53473, D53475, AB2300A, AB3399A, D68529, E04182, E04179, AB3282A, AB2590B, AB3291A, AB3285B, AB3288A, AB2343A, AB3473A, AB3401A, D49753, D53474, AB2142A, AB3150A, AB3137A and AB3395A of Trayenta (linagliptin) 5 mg in a bottle with 30 tablets, which were marketed to the public health sector with analytical certificates.
title: Check Diabetes type 2 medicine

text: counterfeit.
title: Check Diabetes type 2 medicine

text: Additionally, lots D74364 and D7436 with an expiration date of 12/31/2024 of Trayenta Duo (linagliptin/metformin) 2.5mg/850mg,Also marketed to the public health sector, it has irregularities on the blister, since its printing is erased when a solvent is applied.
title: Check Diabetes type 2 medicine

text: Why we "migrate" to Mexico.....
title: Snowbirds

text: Deleted by owner
title: One Upsmanship

text: We've been here 26 years, and at that time there was a two-lane highway from Chapala to Jocotepec, with lots of varying crops food from AJijic to SJC. This recent (assume) drone pic from a little west of SJC   and angling to Chapala is absolutely horrifying...... from density to water use to overcrowding to loss of chayote fields and others.
title: Two Lane HIghway is the Only Remaining Constant

text: But the most damning of all is there is STILL only a TWO-LANE highway....... better maintained, etc. but  no more traffic lanes.
title: Two Lane HIghway is the Only Remaining Constant

text: So anyone who says traffic "isn't that bad" or "YES, we need more developments" is totally delusional and needs only to consider same. 😬
title: Two Lane HIghway is the Only Remaining Constant

text: Some may say hooey to 5g health effects, but my provider in the U.S. advised to turn off the phone at night, and to not have the modem in the bedroom. In other words, limit too close exposure to wireless 5g signal, or don't be where it is over concentrated, real strong.
title: Health Reasons To Choose Telmex Fiber Over TotalPlay?

text: Of course this goes against the current freedoms where everyone is using phones all day and using wifi in the home, and presumably few are using cat cable to hook up to modems. However, using cat cable from modem to a PC, or from modem to a TV, perhaps the most used items all day long at home, might make sense in limiting 5g signal exposure.
title: Health Reasons To Choose Telmex Fiber Over TotalPlay?

text: If any of this has merit, which provider, Telmex Fiber or TotalPlay Fiber offers the modem with multiple cable connection possibilities? Or do they both? Which one offers modem with ability to limit or even turn off the 5g signal? Anybody care to comment. Much appreciated, as I need to install one or the other this week.
title: Health Reasons To Choose Telmex Fiber Over TotalPlay?

text: In Mexico September is known as the month of wills.
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: Who needs a Mexican will?  Anybody that owns property, cars, bank accounts, investment accounts and similar in Mexico. A will from a foreign country will not be valid in Mexico,
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: Notarios (the only attorneys that can legally execute a will) offer discounts up to 50% of their normal fees.
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: The average price in Jalisco as reported by PROFECO  in September is $2150
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: Go to the notary of your choice:
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: Bring official identification: It is essential that you present a valid official identification, such as your INE, passport or military card.
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: Provide your general information: Once at the notary's office, you must provide your personal information so that the notary can record it in the document.
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: Have witnesses: In some cases, the notary may request the presence of witnesses to attest to the act. Make sure you have them available if necessary.
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: In September there are reduced prices for the procedure.
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: Write down your wishes and register your heirs: Finally, you must clearly and precisely express how you want your assets to be distributed and who your heirs will be.
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: If you have more questions regarding this process, you can visit the official website of the Government of Mexico:
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: https://www.gob.mx/testamento/articulos/tengo-que-preparar-una-lista-de-mis-bienes-para-hacer-testamento-mesdeltestamento
title: September is the month for wills (testamentos)

text: Does anyone have experience with grounding pads or grounding sheets for bed?  I'd like to get some.
title: Grounding Earthing Mats or Sheets

text: Grounding (or earthing as it's also called) has worked amazingly well for me 👾 (and my wife)... and the results have been noticed by friends. I broke my leg and ankle 6 years ago in a Canadian Pacific Railroad yard accident that caused me residual nerve issues, pain, swelling and discoloration in my leg.
title: Grounding Earthing Mats or Sheets

text: Grounding relieved that pain, discomfort and improved my tissues over the past 6 months since we started it. Helped me sleep better at night without the pain.
title: Grounding Earthing Mats or Sheets

text: Will grounding work for you? Who knows? The benefits can be huge or subtle but it's always free. What have you got to loose except 20-30 mins a day sitting with your feet in the grass reading a book or magazine? (I wouldn't use your phone or laptop while grounding, kind of defeats the purpose.)
title: Grounding Earthing Mats or Sheets

text: Here's a link to the video we first watched that got our attention. Buena suerte!
title: Grounding Earthing Mats or Sheets

text: From El Informador
title: The Chapálico Sea:  what Lake Chapala looked like in the past

text: Once considered a "sea" by the first Spaniards
title: The Chapálico Sea:  what Lake Chapala looked like in the past

text: According to the Government of Jalisco, Chapala means  “place of small pots or jars” (Nahuatl); “very wet or soaked place” (coca), or  “place of grasshoppers on the water”  (Nahuatl).
title: The Chapálico Sea:  what Lake Chapala looked like in the past

text: Plano del Lago de Chapala de Don José María Narváez (Diciembre 1816).
title: The Chapálico Sea:  what Lake Chapala looked like in the past

text: I've been using HSBC since CiBanco turned into cash/card thieves. NEVER an issue before. Today two withdrawals... one on hubbies' card, other on mine. His was fine. They always have been. Using my card, receipt said I got what I requested but machine dispensed $500 pesos less. Got a teller to run thru her counting machine. Yep..$500 still short.
title: ATM at HSBC Shorted me Today

text: Bank guy said it was up to me to call my (Canadian) bank as have them initiate an enquiry. Done. Now waiting for refund of approx $40 to my bank account there.
title: ATM at HSBC Shorted me Today

text: Only bringing this up becasue some have had issues with various machines in the area lately, but as much as I despise HSBC as a bank, have never once had a problem with their ATM's.  I used machine # 138181 so (1)   say you DO want a printed receipt, and (2) count whatever comes out!
title: ATM at HSBC Shorted me Today

text: You know you are Canadian.....
title: HAPPY CANADA DAY ! ! ! !

text: If your local Dairy Queen is closed from September through May, you may live in Canada. If someone in a Home Depot store offers you assistance and they don't work there, you may live in Canada. If you've worn shorts and a parka at the same time, you may live in Canada. If you've had a lengthy telephone conversation with someone who dialed a wrong number, you may live in Canada.
title: HAPPY CANADA DAY ! ! ! !

text: If 'Vacation' means going anywhere south of Detroit for the weekend, you may live in Canada. If you measure distance in hours, you may live in Canada.
title: HAPPY CANADA DAY ! ! ! !

text:
If you know several people who have hit a deer more than once, you may live in Canada
	
	If you have switched from 'heat' to 'A/C' in the same day and back again, you may live in Canada
	
	If you can drive 90 kms/hr through 2 feet of snow during a raging blizzard without flinching, you may live in Canada.
title: HAPPY CANADA DAY ! ! ! !

text: If you install security lights on your house and garage, but leave both unlocked, you may live in Canada. If you carry jumpers in your car and your wife knows how to use them, you may live in Canada. If you design your kid's Halloween costume to fit over a snowsuit, you may live in Canada.
title: HAPPY CANADA DAY ! ! ! !

text: If the speed limit on the highway is 80km -- you're going 90 and everybody is passing you, you may live in Canada. If driving is better in the winter because the potholes are filled with snow, you may live in Canada.
title: HAPPY CANADA DAY ! ! ! !

text:
If you know all 4 seasons: almost winter, winter, still winter, and road construction, you may live in Canada
	
	If you have more miles on your snow blower than your car, you may live in Canada. If you find 2 degrees 'a little chilly', you may live in Canada.
title: HAPPY CANADA DAY ! ! ! !

text: Thanks,  Jeff FOXWORTHY
title: HAPPY CANADA DAY ! ! ! !

text: During the past week the massive traffic problem where passengers cross the path of cars dropping people off was solved.
title: New Sky Bridge at Guadalajara Airport

text: After parking your car, you have to go to level 3 and cross over the road using the new enclosed bridge, go down a couple of escalators where you're near the international arrival area.
title: New Sky Bridge at Guadalajara Airport

text: The taxi stands were moved to a spot under the escalator.
title: New Sky Bridge at Guadalajara Airport

text: This improvement makes passenger drop off and pick up a lot quicker.
title: New Sky Bridge at Guadalajara Airport

text: The only problem is that there are only 2 payment machines on level 3 lined up with people waiting to pay while on level 1 there are 6 cajeros with no wait. That will probably change soon.
title: New Sky Bridge at Guadalajara Airport

text: Our own weekly local newspaper is now being published completely bilingually.  The reportage is excellent and timely. It is a good way to improve your understanding of Spanish and not miss out on the local news if your language skills are not yet perfected. On sale every Saturday around town or at their office on Colon next to Lilifher Bakery/Deli.
title: Semanario Laguna Newspaper Now Completely Bilingual!

text:
5:19 am
	Well, I've been up since 3 am. Torrential rain and the sump pump (which I had put in my "rain barrel" aka 121 litre garbage can) would not shut off. I have been just rolling over and going back to sleep when I hear it. Then I heard the water overflowing the barrel in addition to the noise from the sump pump, so, I got up to investigate. OMG!
title: Torrential rain early this morning

text: The entire center courtyard was filling up with water. I would not have been able to pull the sump pump out of barrel without getting soaking wet and watched in horror as the water level crept to within a half inch of the bottom of the kitchen door. Finally, around 4 am, the rain eased up and the sump pump could keep up.
title: Torrential rain early this morning

text: I waded out, grabbed the pump and dropped it into the real inground receptacle that I just had made for it and watched while it made short work of my new wading pool. Interesting that it made hardly any noise at all by comparison to the racket in the above ground barrel. I love the idea of collecting the rain water but... It's now 5:20 a.m. Cora wants breakfast and I need a coffee... badly.
title: Torrential rain early this morning

text: I think it's going to be a multiple nap day. 5:43 a.m. Okay, got some coffee in me and realizing that I had better have a back up sump pump because, if this one ever fails (and they do) then I'm fucked. When the three houses were built beside me, they cut off my drain from the centre atrium into what was a field.
title: Torrential rain early this morning

text: I had assumed, incorrectly, that the pipe drained to the front of my house and out to the street but the builder also neglected to tell me what they had done. I think (you can translate that as "hope") that I have now solved the problem by digging a sump and putting the pump where it should have been in the first place.
title: Torrential rain early this morning

text: The sump pump delivers the water to the front section of my roof, into the gutter, down the spout and out to the street. Let it pour!!! Hope everyone has a damn, fine, splendid day! LOL.
title: Torrential rain early this morning

text: It's always a good idea to collect rainwater but, right now, there's no rain. However, there are many air conditioners going full tilt with this extended heat and they all are producing water as part of their dehumidify and cooling cycle. How much? I gotta tell you I was surprised. In one week, from one 1 ton mini split, I recovered a FULL large outdoor garbage container of free water.
title: Free water for your garden

text: Upon further investigation, the mini split is producing two bucketfuls daily. My plants are very happy.
title: Free water for your garden

text: I have an overhead fan in my bedroom and it  began slowing up and just making believe it was cooling. I hunted for new capasiters (spelling) and called my trusty electrician, plumber, brain surgeon  man and he replaced the old units with new ones and now my fan thinks it is new again. I was, and am, outraged by the 400 mx peso charge for two men....but I  will learn to live with it.
title: Ceiling fan FIX

text: That is the same charge for my lobotomy so he is still holding his prices in line......and no Guiness, I am partially kidding    Mexico is still a huge bargain.
title: Ceiling fan FIX

text: This post is not political but rather informational, and any posts that are deemed political will be removed by the mods so please show restraint I strongly feel that voting is a responsibility and a right for every eligible citizen. If you are a U.S. citizen, you must register in order to receive a ballot even if you are already registered to vote.
title: U.S. Citizens must register to vote each year

text: Absentee ballots for overseas voters have different rules and procedures than regular absentee ballots. The Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act (UOCAVA) requires that US citizens living abroad register every year and request a ballot in order to vote during that year. By law the state must send each registered overseas voter a ballot 45 days before each election.
title: U.S. Citizens must register to vote each year

text: All registered overseas voters are allowed to vote in Federal elections and some states allow them to vote in state and local elections as well. Each state, and some local counties, have their own rules so it is important to know the rules for the state where you will be voting. All states must offer the voter application and ballot be delivered electronically (recommended) by fax or by mail.
title: U.S. Citizens must register to vote each year

text: Most states allow the form to be returned electronically or by fax but some require return by mail.
title: U.S. Citizens must register to vote each year

text: There are several Websites where you can register as an overseas voter. The federal government site is Federal Voting Assistance Program. I recommend using the Vote From Abroad.org site because they have a more robust step-by-step interface and offer on-line help.
title: U.S. Citizens must register to vote each year

text:
First you need to verify that you are still registered to vote. States purge their voter rolls for a variety of reasons so check first to be sure you are still registered.
		
	
	
		
			Request a Federal Post Card Application (FPCA) from your local election official.
		
	
	
		
			You will need to provide the following information:
title: U.S. Citizens must register to vote each year

text:
Full name, US voting address and ZIP code.
	
	
		Your current, non-US address.
	
	
		Your social security number (some state only request the last 4 digits)
	
	
		Possibly your state ID (if you have one)
	
	
		Political party (not required but necessary in some states to vote in the primary)
title: U.S. Citizens must register to vote each year

text:
Complete, verify the information and return the form to your local election official.
		
	
	
		
			Confirm that the form has been received. You should receive conformation that the form has been received and approved within a week. If not, contact your local election official. If there are problems, use the VoteFromAbroad Website to help you resolve them.
title: U.S. Citizens must register to vote each year

text: According to a Reuters article this morning, 2023 was the hottest year on record world wide. In Chapala. the temperature climbed a little above 37 Celsius. I finally gave up and went to Mazamitla for 10days. Am making plans to spend several months in the Metepec area this summer  because a seriously doubt we will see any improvement this year. To bad many do not believe in climate change.
title: 2023 Hottest year on record
